sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: This Round 32 clash in Ligue 1 sees Toulouse, currently 12th with 38 points, host Stade Rennais, who are just one position above in 11th with the same 38 points. The game at Stadium de Toulouse promises a tight battle between two mid-table teams desperate for points. With both teams level on points, the outcome could significantly impact their final standings. Referee Benoît Millot, known for averaging 3.9 yellow cards and 0.29 red cards per game, will oversee this encounter in the city of Toulouse.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these sides, Stade Rennais has dominated with 3 wins, while Toulouse managed 1 victory, and 1 game ended in a draw. However, Toulouse has been unbeaten in the last 4 head-to-head encounters, showing resilience against their rivals. Their most recent meeting on November 10, 2024, saw Toulouse triumph 2-0 away at Stade Rennais, with Aron Dønnum earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive Sofascore rating of 8. Historically, matches between these teams tend to have low card counts, with 5 out of 7 games seeing fewer than 4.5 cards, and 4 out of 5 having under 10.5 corners.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Toulouse
sections.text: Toulouse will rely on players like Aron Dønnum, who has a standout average Sofascore rating of 7.43 in the last 5 matchups against Stade Rennais, including 1 goal and 3 starts. Midfielder Vincent Sierro brings creativity with 5 goals and an average rating of 7.01, while Warren Kamanzi stands out defensively, boasting a league-leading 100% aerial duel win rate. Forwards Frank Magri and Joshua King, both with 5 goals each, will be crucial in attack, despite some underperformance against expected goals (xG) metrics. Unfortunately, Toulouse will miss key forward Zakaria Aboukhlal, who has 7 goals this season.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Stade Rennais
sections.text: Stade Rennais boasts firepower in Arnaud Kalimuendo, who ranks 4th in the league with 15 goals and has been in form with 3 goals and 3 assists in the last 5 matches, holding a Sofascore rating of 7.42. Ludovic Blas adds dynamism with 6 goals and 7 assists, complemented by an average rating of 7.22. Defender Lilian Brassier, with a league rank of 5 for aerial duels won at 75.4%, will be vital at the back. Adrien Truffert, also in fine form with a recent average rating of 7.7, contributes both defensively and offensively. However, they’ll miss defender Alidu Seidu, who ranks 2nd league-wide for ground duels won at 71.6% and 1st for fouls drawn per game at 2.5.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Toulouse is enduring a rough patch, winless in their last 7 general matches, though they have a tendency to see both teams scoring in 5 of their last 7 home games. They’ve also conceded first in 4 of their last 5 home matches, which could be a concern. Stade Rennais, on the other hand, hasn’t kept a clean sheet in their last 4 away games and has seen over 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 away matches. Both teams have shown consistency in low-corner games against each other, with 4 of 5 head-to-heads having fewer than 10.5 corners.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Stats
sections.text: Toulouse’s season stats reveal strengths in defense, ranking 7th for least goals conceded (40) and 3rd for clean sheets (10), while offensively they rank 11th with 40 goals scored. Their penalty area entries per game rank impressively at 5th with 29.09, showing attacking intent, though their big chance conversion rate of 36.25% ranks 13th. Stade Rennais matches Toulouse’s goal tally at 40, with a strong late-game scoring trend, netting 23.91% of their goals in the 76-90 minute period. Toulouse, conversely, peaks early, with 27.5% of goals between the 16th and 30th minutes. These timing differences could dictate tactical approaches in this match.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts
sections.text: Toulouse has a notable league rank of 3rd for the percentage of goals assisted at 75%, highlighting their teamwork in attack. They also rank 4th for overperformance in goals versus expected goals with a difference of 5.143, showing clinical finishing. Stade Rennais’s Arnaud Kalimuendo is a standout, ranking 4th in the league for goals scored with 15, making him a constant threat. Toulouse’s Warren Kamanzi holds a unique stat, leading the league with a perfect 100% aerial duel win rate. Additionally, Toulouse ranks 3rd for the fewest yellow cards per game at 1.625, suggesting discipline despite committing the 5th most fouls in the league at 417.
